title: How to Use YouTube's New Thumbnail Test & Compare Feature for Better Engagement
excerpt: Explains YouTube's Thumbnail Test & Compare feature and how creators can use it to improve engagement with better thumbnail decisions.
contentHtml:
Transkript

hey everyone exciting news YouTube is finally rolling out thumbnail AB testing or as they call it thumbnail test and compare starting day June 3rd about 50% of creators will have access to the feature and the rest is going to have access in the coming weeks so what is thumbnail test and compare to put it simply it's a feature that lets you upload up to three different thumbnails for your videos to see which one performs best with your audience this is often called AB testing but because

there is a c option YouTube opted for the name test and compare how does the test and compare work once you have access to the feature you see the option test and compare at places where you can choose your thumbnail that could be during uploads or with already uploaded videos in the details tab so you have to go to content select a video and then you have the option to change the thumbnail and run a test YouTube will show The Chosen thumbnails evenly across your videos

viewers and after some time they will give you a winner uh which is based on the thumbnail that generated the mo most watchtime share why watchtime share YouTube optimizes overall watchtime share to ensure the thumbnail and content get the right viewer satisfaction and Vier engagement a high click rate only would mean like incentivizing clickbait and that that wouldn't lead to much satisfied viewers and therefore uh hinder growth on your channel it might take a few days or even up to two weeks to get finalized test

results especially if you're a smaller Creator so The Impressions and views need to rack up before YouTube can uh can give you an a final result and sometimes you might not even get a clear winner result but don't worry it's all part of the process you'll see a running status if a test is still in progress make sure your thumbnails have significant differences that means test different layouts backgrounds text overlays and uh if you're still not getting a winner that this might be due to low

Impressions so the thumbnail isn't getting shown as much around uh or maybe the thumbnails are equally good so that can also be a possibility once the test is complete you'll get a report with the results if a thumbnail clearly outperforms others it will be labeled as a winner and YouTube will automatically update your video to use only that thumbnail if there's no clear winner the first thumbnail will be shown to your audience remember you always have control even if there's a preferred or no clear winner

you can manually set the thumbnails you want to use who can use thumbnail test and compare so to use thumbnail test and compare you need to have have advanced features enabled on your channel whether or not you have that you can check in your channel settings under feature eligibility also I've linked to you the help article on how to get Advanced features down in the video description test and compare is only available currently on YouTube desktop Studio but YouTube is exploring ways to support it on

mobile devices in the future also you can only test thumbnails on public long form videos live stream archives or podcast episodes it is not available for videos that that are made for kids made for mature audiences or private videos so that's all for now from my perspective if you have questions please leave them in the comments below I can't wait to hear what you have to say about the thumbnail testing compare feature and see how it helps your channel grow um let us know in the

comments if you have already access or if you've seen improvements through testing uh that would be Splendid and we'll see us in the next video take care bye
